objective: Create internal/generate/integration_test.go (white-box `package generate`) — the PRD §20.1 layer-3 end-to-end integration suite that drives the REAL generate.CommitStaged (P1.M6.T1.S1) against a REAL temp git repository + the REAL stub-agent binary (P1.M6.T3.S1) across ALL seven contract paths: SUCCESS, DUP-RETRY-THEN-SUCCESS, PARSE-FAIL-THEN-RESCUE, TIMEOUT, CAS-FAILURE (HEAD moved mid-run), ROOT-COMMIT (unborn repo), and NOTHING-STAGED. The REAL *git.Git is wired into Deps.Git and the REAL *provider.Executor into Deps.Runner, with NewStubManifest providing the stub provider, so every assertion is made against real git plumbing output (git cat-file/show/log/rev-parse) and real process execution — proving the snapshot-based atomic-commit core (decisions.md §8; PRD §13 + §13.5 edge cases). The suite's OUTPUT feeds the §18.1 invariant assertions in P1.M6.T3.S3. Scope: the integration suite ONLY — CommitStaged, the stub binary, and the temp-repo primitives already exist; do NOT modify them.
implementationSteps: CREATE internal/generate/integration_test.go as white-box `package generate` (so it can name the package-private gitClient/runner interfaces and compose the in-package stub helpers). Imports: bytes, context, errors, os/exec, path/filepath, strings, testing, time, plus internal/config, internal/git, internal/provider, internal/ui. NO testify, NO real LLM. Add a file-level doc comment naming PRD §20.1 layer 3 + decisions.md §8.

Implement the raw-git BOOTSTRAP helpers that *git.Git does not expose (Decision A — the internal/git harness is white-box `package git`, unreachable from package generate; mirror its conventions exactly): gitRun(t,dir,args...) string (exec.Command("git",args...).Dir=dir, capture stdout+stderr, t.Fatalf on error); newTempRepo(t) string (t.TempDir + `git init -q` + repo-LOCAL `config user.email stagehand@example.com`, `config user.name Stagehand Test`, `config commit.gpgsign false`); writeStage(t,dir,path,content) (os.WriteFile after MkdirAll on the parent, then `git add <path>`); seedCommit(t,dir,msg) (writeStage a unique fileN.txt then `git commit -q -m <msg>` with the WHOLE message as one -m arg).

Implement e2eDeps(t,dir,script,cfg,stdout,stderr) Deps: real *git.Git (git.New(dir)) → Deps.Git; real *provider.Executor (provider.NewExecutor("")) → Deps.Runner; NewStubManifest(t, StubConfig{Script: script, StateFile: filepath.Join(t.TempDir(),"c")}) → Deps.Manifest (ALWAYS set a StateFile — required for multi-entry scripts, harmless otherwise); cfg → Deps.Config; ui.NewOutput(stdout,stderr,false,true) → Deps.Output (noColor=true so out.Red is a no-op).

Implement the deterministic headMoverRunner decorator for the CAS test (Decision C): type headMoverRunner struct { inner runner; dir string; moved bool } implementing the package-private runner interface. Run: if !r.moved, advance HEAD via PLUMBING on the current HEAD's tree (strings.TrimSpace(gitRun(r.dir,"rev-parse","HEAD^{tree}")) → tree; rev-parse HEAD → parent; commit-tree -p <parent> -m "concurrent commit elsewhere" <tree> → newc; update-ref HEAD <newc>), set r.moved=true, then return r.inner.Run(...). Parse: delegate to r.inner.Parse(raw,m). CRITICAL: use HEAD^{tree} (NOT the index) so the staged file is UNTOUCHED — never `git commit --allow-empty` (it would commit the staged file and corrupt the snapshot).

Implement TestIntegration_Success: newTempRepo + seedCommit("feat: baseline") + writeStage("feature.go","package x
"); capture oldHead := TrimSpace(gitRun("rev-parse","HEAD")); SCRIPT [{Emit:"feat: add feature module

Implements the new feature."}]; cfg=config.Default(). Assert: err==nil; gitRun("cat-file","-t",res.CommitSHA)=="commit"; the ^tree <hash>$ line parsed from gitRun("cat-file","-p",res.CommitSHA) == TrimSpace(gitRun("write-tree")); the ^parent <hash>$ line == oldHead; TrimSpace(gitRun("log","-1","--format=%B",res.CommitSHA)) == res.Message; stdout contains "["+res.CommitSHA[:7]+"] feat: add feature module"; stderr has NO rescue block ("Commit generation failed").

Implement TestIntegration_DupRetryThenSuccess: seedCommit("feat: dup") + writeStage("feature.go",...); SCRIPT [{Emit:"feat: dup"},{Emit:"feat: unique module"}]; cfg=config.Default(). Assert: err==nil; res.Subject=="feat: unique module"; TrimSpace(gitRun("log","-1","--format=%B",res.CommitSHA)) contains "feat: unique module"; HEAD advanced by exactly one commit over the seeded history.

Implement TestIntegration_ParseFailThenRescue: writeStage("feature.go",...) (history optional); SCRIPT [{Emit:""},{Emit:""}] — EMPTY on both entries (Decision D: parseOutput ok = TrimSpace(msg)!=""; a non-empty "garbage" would parse ok=true and commit, NOT rescue); cfg=config.Default(). Assert: errors.Is(err,ErrRescue); stderr contains the rescue block ("Commit generation failed", "Tree ID:"); NO commit created (res.CommitSHA==""); HEAD unchanged (rev-parse before==after); index unchanged (`git diff --cached --name-only` before==after).

Implement TestIntegration_Timeout: writeStage("feature.go",...); SCRIPT [{Hang:true}]; cfg=config.Default() with cfg.Timeout=500*time.Millisecond. Assert: errors.Is(err,ErrRescue) (the executor returns *provider.TimeoutError which wraps context.DeadlineExceeded, NOT context.Canceled, so CommitStaged renders Rescue("")+returns ErrRescue); stderr contains the rescue block; NO commit; HEAD + index unchanged.

Implement TestIntegration_HeadMovedCASFailure: seedCommit("feat: baseline") + writeStage("feature.go",...); capture beforeHead; build Deps INLINE with Runner: &headMoverRunner{inner: provider.NewExecutor(""), dir: dir} (not the bare executor e2eDeps wires), Manifest from NewStubManifest with SCRIPT [{Emit:"feat: ok message"}], cfg=config.Default(). Assert: errors.Is(err,ErrHeadMoved); stderr contains the §13.5 message ("HEAD moved", "aborting to avoid a non-fast-forward", "Your generated message was:", "git commit-tree", "xargs git update-ref HEAD") AND does NOT contain the rescue block; HEAD after == the decorator's concurrent commit (rev-parse HEAD) and != beforeHead and != res.CommitSHA (NEVER forced); index unchanged (`git diff --cached --name-only` before==after).

Implement TestIntegration_RootCommit: newTempRepo and LEAVE IT UNBORN (seedCommit NOTHING) + writeStage("README.md","hello\n"); SCRIPT [{Emit:"feat: initial commit"}]; cfg=config.Default(). Assert: err==nil; the gitRun("cat-file","-p",res.CommitSHA) output has NO ^parent line (root commit); its ^tree line == TrimSpace(gitRun("write-tree")); TrimSpace(gitRun("rev-parse","HEAD")) == res.CommitSHA (HEAD advanced from unborn to the new commit).

Implement TestIntegration_NothingStaged: newTempRepo + seedCommit("feat: baseline") and DO NOT stage anything (clean index); SCRIPT [{Emit:"ignored"}] (never reached); cfg=config.Default(). Assert: errors.Is(err,ErrNothingToCommit); stderr has NO rescue block; HEAD unchanged; index unchanged; and (the v2-seam point) generate performed NO auto-stage — StagedDiff returned "" so CommitStaged short-circuited before WriteTree (no dangling tree).

RUN the validation gates in order and fix every failure before declaring done: go build ./internal/generate/ ; go vet ./internal/generate/ ; test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/generate/)" ; go build ./internal/generate/testdata/stubagent ; go test ./internal/generate/ -run Integration -v ; go test ./...

Docs: this task has no Mode-A per-item DOCS line (it is a test-only artifact); it defers to the Mode-B changeset-level doc sync performed in M8 (README/docs). No doc file edit is required in this task. The suite's OUTPUT feeds the §18.1 invariant assertions in P1.M6.T3.S3.

validationGates.level: 1 — build the generate package (incl. new test file)
2 — build the stub binary the suite execs
4 — full module regression (existing unit + stub self-tests unaffected)

successCriteria.description: internal/generate/integration_test.go exists, is white-box `package generate`, and uses only stdlib + internal/{config,git,provider,ui} + the in-package stub helpers (BuildStubBinary/NewStubManifest/StubConfig/StubResponse) — NO testify, NO new external deps, NO real LLM.

The suite wires the REAL *git.Git (git.New(dir)) into Deps.Git and the REAL *provider.Executor (provider.NewExecutor("")) into Deps.Runner with a stub Manifest from NewStubManifest and a config from config.Default(), so the full real pipeline (executor execs the stub binary → stdout → parse → real git plumbing) is exercised end-to-end.

Repo bootstrap uses a local raw-git helper via os/exec (gitRun/newTempRepo/writeStage/seedCommit) that MIRRORS internal/git/gittestutil_test.go conventions exactly (TempDir; git init -q; repo-local user.email/user.name/commit.gpgsign=false) — because *git.Git does not expose init/add/commit and the internal/git harness is white-box `package git` (unreachable from package generate).
All seven contract paths are covered as separate subtests matching -run Integration: SUCCESS; DUP-RETRY-THEN-SUCCESS; PARSE-FAIL-THEN-RESCUE; TIMEOUT; CAS-FAILURE; ROOT-COMMIT; NOTHING-STAGED.

TestIntegration_Success asserts via REAL git: git cat-file -t <sha> == commit; the tree line parsed from git cat-file -p <sha> == git write-tree of the unchanged index; the parent line == the old HEAD captured pre-run; git log -1 --format=%B <sha> == Result.Message; stdout contains [<sha[:7]>] <subject>; stderr has no rescue block.

TestIntegration_DupRetryThenSuccess uses a stub script that emits a duplicate subject then a unique one and asserts the created commit's message is the unique subject and HEAD advanced by exactly one commit.

TestIntegration_ParseFailThenRescue uses StubResponse{Emit:""} on both inner-try entries (parseOutput returns ok=false ONLY for empty output under raw mode; a non-empty "garbage" would commit), asserts errors.Is(err,ErrRescue), the rescue block in stderr, no commit created, and HEAD + `git diff --cached --name-only` unchanged.

TestIntegration_Timeout uses StubResponse{Hang:true} with a short cfg.Timeout and asserts errors.Is(err,ErrRescue) (the executor returns *provider.TimeoutError, not context.Canceled), the rescue block in stderr, no commit, and HEAD + index unchanged.

TestIntegration_HeadMovedCASFailure moves HEAD DURING Runner.Run via a deterministic headMoverRunner decorator that advances HEAD with plumbing on HEAD^{tree} (never `git commit --allow-empty`, which would corrupt the staged index), and asserts errors.Is(err,ErrHeadMoved), the §13.5 message in stderr, NO rescue block, HEAD == the decorator's commit (NEVER force-updated to the stagehand commit), and the index unchanged.

TestIntegration_RootCommit uses an UNBORN repo (no seedCommit), asserts the created commit's git cat-file -p output has NO parent line, its tree line == git write-tree, and git rev-parse HEAD == Result.CommitSHA (advanced from unborn).

TestIntegration_NothingStaged stages nothing, asserts errors.Is(err,ErrNothingToCommit) at the GENERATE layer (the CLI, not generate, performs auto-stage-all — v2 seam), no rescue block, HEAD + index unchanged, and no snapshot taken (StagedDiff returned "" so WriteTree was never reached).

Every FAILURE path (parse-fail, timeout, CAS-failure, nothing-staged) asserts the §18.1-style invariants against real git: HEAD unchanged (git rev-parse HEAD before==after) and index unchanged (git diff --cached --name-only before==after).
No shipped file is modified by this task: generate.go, stubprovider_test.go, testdata/stubagent/main.go, and every internal/git file are untouched (the suite consumes them as-is).

go build ./internal/generate/, go vet ./internal/generate/, gofmt (internal/generate/ clean), go build ./internal/generate/testdata/stubagent, go test ./internal/generate/ -run Integration -v, and go test ./... all pass.

Docs: task has no Mode-A DOCS line (test-only artifact); defers to Mode-B changeset-level doc sync in M8 — no doc file edit required here. The suite's OUTPUT feeds the §18.1 invariant assertions in P1.M6.T3.S3.

references: plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M6_T3_S2_research.md — the 4 design decisions (raw-git setup, white-box pkg, deterministic CAS decorator, empty-not-garbage parse-fail) + verified signatures + the 7-path table + assertion mechanics
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §8 — the integration-test seam (stub provider drives CommitStaged e2e against a temp repo; covers all paths)
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md §1 (the COMMIT block + trap timing) and §5 (the rescue message baseline)

internal/generate/generate.go — CommitStaged(ctx,deps Deps)(Result,error); Deps{Git gitClient;Runner runner;Manifest;Config;Output}; Result{CommitSHA,Subject,Message}; sentinels ErrNothingToCommit/ErrRescue/ErrHeadMoved; gitClient+runner are PACKAGE-PRIVATE (why this test must be package generate)

internal/generate/stubprovider_test.go — BuildStubBinary(t)/NewStubManifest(t,StubConfig)/StubConfig{Script,StateFile,StdinLog}/StubResponse{Emit,Hang,Fail} (precedence Hang>Fail>0>Emit); the helpers this suite composes
internal/generate/testdata/stubagent/main.go — the fake-agent binary: reads STAGEHAND_STUB_SCRIPT/_STATE/_STDIN; selects script[min(N,len-1)] (clamp); advances the counter before acting

internal/git/gittestutil_test.go — the repo-bootstrap conventions to MIRROR (TempDir; git init -q; repo-local user.email/user.name/commit.gpgsign=false); WHITE-BOX package git so unreachable from package generate
internal/git/git.go — git.New(dir) (*Git,error) (LookPath at construction); *Git funnels through unexported g.run

internal/git/plumbing.go — RevParseHEAD (unborn ⇒ ("",false,nil))/WriteTree/CommitTree (omits -p when parent=="")/UpdateRefCAS (root: expected=="")/DiffTreeNameStatus — the real methods Deps.Git calls
internal/git/diff.go — StagedDiff(git.DiffSettings) returns ("",nil) on an empty index (FR5) — the NOTHING-STAGED trigger

internal/provider/executor.go — provider.NewExecutor(dir); Run returns *TimeoutError (wraps DeadlineExceeded)/*AgentError/bare context.Canceled; Parse(raw,m) is the exported seam over package-private parseOutput
internal/provider/parse.go — parseOutput returns ok = (TrimSpace(msg)!="") (line 75); raw output ⇒ non-empty parses ok=true (why PARSE-FAIL must use Emit="")
internal/config/defaults.go — config.Default() ⇒ Timeout=120s, MaxDuplicateRetries=3, MaxDiffBytes=300000, MaxMdLines=100, SubjectTargetChars=50

PRD.md §13 (snapshot atomic-commit core), §13.5 (edge cases: rootless + HEAD-moved message), §18.1 (the invariant), §18.2 (failure-mode table), §18.3 (rescue block), §18.4 (signal handling), §20.1 layer 3 (integration w/ stub provider), §20.2 (invariant tests)
https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec#Command — exec.Command("git",args...).Dir=dir for the raw-git bootstrap helper (Decision A)
https://git-scm.com/docs/git-commit-tree — commit-tree on HEAD^{tree} to advance HEAD without touching the index (the headMover decorator, Decision C)
